evals.prompt:
Read the existing Drupal module at /tmp/phase17-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/ to understand its current code. This module has Project and Task content entities (entity type IDs: 'project' and 'task') with CRUD operations, access control handlers with granular permissions (view/edit own/edit any/delete own/delete any for both entity types), a settings form at /admin/config/group_ai_pm/settings, a dashboard controller at /admin/group-ai-pm/dashboard, a ProjectStatusBlock block plugin, Views integration with a computed ProjectTaskCount views field, hook_cron for overdue task detection with Lock API, and a QueueWorker for processing overdue notifications.

Write comprehensive PHPUnit tests for this module:

1. **Kernel Test for Entity CRUD**: Test that Project and Task entities can be created, saved, loaded, and deleted. Verify field defaults work correctly (Project status defaults to 'planning', Task status defaults to 'todo', Task priority defaults to 'medium'). Test that a Task's 'project' entity reference field correctly links to a parent Project.

2. **Kernel Test for Access Control**: Test the access control handlers. Create test users with specific permissions like 'view project', 'edit own project', 'edit any project', 'delete own task', 'delete any task'. Verify that checkAccess returns correct allow/deny results: a user with 'edit own project' can edit their own project but not someone else's; a user with 'edit any project' can edit any project; a user without 'view project' cannot view projects.

3. **Functional Test for Settings Form**: Test that the settings form requires the 'administer group_ai_pm' permission (anonymous user gets 403). Test that an authorized admin user can load the form (200), submit it with custom values for default_project_status and default_task_status, and verify the configuration was saved.

4. **Functional Test for Dashboard**: Test that the dashboard page requires proper permissions. Verify that an authorized user sees the dashboard with project information displayed.

5. **Kernel Test for Cron and Queue**: Test the overdue task detection chain. Create a Project, then create a Task with a due_date in the past and status 'todo'. Run cron via the cron service. Verify that the overdue notification queue has items after cron runs. Also test that the OverdueNotificationWorker QueueWorker plugin can process an item by directly instantiating or loading it from the plugin manager.

Create all test files in /tmp/phase17-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/
After creating all files, run: cd /tmp/phase17-VARIANT && ddev drush cr 2>/dev/null; true
Do NOT ask questions -- just create the code.
evals.expected_output: Test files covering entity CRUD kernel tests, access control kernel tests, settings form functional tests, dashboard functional tests, and cron/queue kernel tests with proper setUp patterns, test discovery annotations, and correct test base classes.
expectations: Every test class has a @group annotation set to 'group_ai_pm' in the class-level PHPDoc comment (the drupal-testing skill explicitly warns: 'Without @group, the test will not be discovered by Drupal test runner or when running phpunit --group module_name' — this is required for test discovery, not optional documentation)

Entity CRUD tests and access control tests use KernelTestBase (NOT BrowserTestBase) as their base class — the testing skill's decision tree says: 'Needs database, services, config, or entities but NOT browser interaction? YES -> Kernel test' — using BrowserTestBase for entity CRUD is 10-100x slower and violates the LOWEST sufficient level principle

Kernel test classes declare a protected static $modules array that includes at minimum 'group_ai_pm', 'system', and 'user' (the testing skill warns: 'Missing modules cause cryptic Service not found or Table not found errors. Include ALL dependency modules your code needs' — 'system' provides core services, 'user' is needed for entity owner references)

Kernel test setUp() calls $this->installEntitySchema('project') and $this->installEntitySchema('task') to create entity tables (the testing skill's WRONG/RIGHT callout: 'Unlike full Drupal installs, Kernel tests do NOT run hook_schema() automatically. Custom tables will not exist, causing Table not found errors' — entity tables are not created automatically in Kernel tests)

Kernel test setUp() calls $this->installEntitySchema('user') to install the user entity schema (both Project and Task entities have entity_reference fields pointing to User entities via 'uid' and 'assignee' fields — saving entities with user references without user schema installed causes foreign key or storage failures)

Kernel test setUp() calls $this->installConfig(['group_ai_pm']) to install the module's default configuration (the settings form test and any code reading group_ai_pm.settings config will fail without installed config — the testing skill shows this as part of the standard Kernel setUp pattern)

Every BrowserTestBase (Functional test) subclass declares protected $defaultTheme = 'stark' or protected $defaultTheme = 'claro' as a class property (the testing skill's WRONG/RIGHT callout: 'BrowserTestBase requires protected $defaultTheme... Without it, tests fail with an unclear error about missing theme')

Every setUp() method in test classes calls parent::setUp() as the FIRST statement before any other setup code (the testing skill's WRONG/RIGHT callout: 'Forgetting to call parent::setUp() or calling it after your setup code... the parent setUp() bootstraps the test environment. Without it, tests fail with cryptic errors')

Test files are placed in the correct directory structure under tests/src/ with subdirectories matching the test type: tests/src/Kernel/ for KernelTestBase tests and tests/src/Functional/ for BrowserTestBase tests (NOT tests/Kernel/, src/Tests/, or any other non-standard path — the testing skill shows the exact required directory layout)

Test class namespaces follow the pattern Drupal\Tests\group_ai_pm\Kernel\ClassName or Drupal\Tests\group_ai_pm\Functional\ClassName matching the directory structure (the testing skill shows: 'tests/src/Kernel/ImporterTest.php -> Drupal\Tests\my_module\Kernel\ImporterTest' — wrong namespace means the class cannot be autoloaded)

Functional settings form test creates a user with 'administer group_ai_pm' permission using $this->drupalCreateUser() and uses $this->drupalLogin() to authenticate before accessing the settings page (the testing skill demonstrates this exact pattern: drupalCreateUser with permission array, then drupalLogin, then drupalGet)

Functional test uses $this->assertSession()->statusCodeEquals(403) to verify access denied for unauthorized users AND $this->assertSession()->statusCodeEquals(200) for authorized users (the testing skill shows this pattern for testing route access control — verifying both the deny and allow cases)

Kernel test $modules array includes 'datetime' module (the Task entity has a due_date field of type 'datetime' which requires the datetime module — the testing skill warns to 'Include ALL dependency modules' and datetime field type is provided by the datetime module, not core)

Cron/queue kernel test creates a Task entity with a past due_date, triggers cron via $this->container->get('cron')->run() or \Drupal::service('cron')->run(), and then asserts the notification queue has items (testing the full hook_cron -> queue pipeline in kernel test context, following the skill's guidance to access services via $this->container->get() rather than static \Drupal:: calls)
